assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration that lacks granular maintenance exclusion types
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'No channel' is legacy and provides detailed comparison table showing it lacks various exclusion types like 'no minor or node upgrades' and 'no minor upgrades'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that 'No channel' does not support 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion scope — only the 30-day 'no upgrades' type, AND per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is also limited
assertions.reason: Clearly states in the comparison table that 'No channel' doesn't support 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion and notes per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is 'Limited (no upgrades 30 days only)'

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Comparison table shows 'Extended support (24 months)' as available on Release Channels but not on No Channel, and mentions Extended channel provides up to 24 months support

assertions.expectation: Notes that rollout sequencing (an advanced feature) is not available on 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Comparison table explicitly shows 'Rollout sequencing (multi-cluster)' as available on Release Channels but not on No Channel

assertions.expectation: Recommends Regular or Stable channel as the closest migration target (legacy behavior matches Stable for minors, Regular for patches)
assertions.reason: Recommends Regular channel as the target configuration and explains it provides 'closest behavior to your current setup' with upgrade timing similar to current Stable-paced minor releases

assertions.expectation: Mentions Extended channel as a migration path for customers who want maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Discusses Extended channel as a future option with up to 24 months of support and notes that minor upgrades are NOT automated, giving complete timing control

assertions.expectation: Warns about exclusion translation: when moving from 'No channel' to release channels, some exclusions don't translate 1:1 and may be ignored — only 'no_upgrades' type translates between the two. Recommends adding a temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion during the transition
assertions.reason: Migration commands show adding temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion before channel migration, then replacing it with persistent exclusion after migration, addressing the translation issue

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud command: 'gcloud container clusters update STAGING_CLUSTER --zone ZONE --release-channel regular'
No reference to the specific Google Cloud documentation URL is provided in the response
